idea.hook: If the Big Bang made matter, it also made ANTI-MATTER.
facts: For every 1,000,000,000 particles of matter, there should've been 1,000,000,000 particles of antimatter.
When matter and antimatter meet, they instantly ANNIHILATE into pure energy.
A perfectly balanced universe would be completely EMPTY – just light and no stars, planets, or YOU.
Yet, our observable universe is overwhelmingly made of matter, with virtually no primordial antimatter.
This implies a tiny 1-in-a-billion excess of matter survived the early universe.
idea.payoff: That microscopic imbalance is why ANYTHING exists at all!
idea.title: Why is there MATTER at all?! 🤯🌌

scenes.visual: A vibrant cosmic nebula swirls, then divides into two distinct, energetic streams: one blue (Matter), one red (Antimatter), moving apart.

scenes.text: 1 Billion Matter vs. 1 Billion Antimatter: Annihilation!
scenes.visual: Numerous blue particles (Matter) and red particles (Antimatter) appear in equal measure, rush towards each other, colliding in brilliant flashes of pure white energy, then disappear.

scenes.text: Perfect balance = EMPTY universe. Just light. No YOU.
scenes.visual: A dark, empty void with only fleeting bright energy flashes. A faint outline of a galaxy or human briefly appears, then dissolves into nothingness, leaving utter darkness.

scenes.text: But our universe is MATTER-DOMINANT. A 1-in-a-billion excess survived.
scenes.visual: A field of blue matter particles dominates the screen. A few red antimatter particles briefly appear and are instantly annihilated. The focus then shifts to a single, distinct blue particle, highlighted.

scenes.text: That tiny imbalance... is why ANYTHING exists!
scenes.visual: Zoom out rapidly from the single blue particle, expanding to show a star, then a planet, then a galaxy, and finally a breathtaking cosmic view of a vibrant universe full of stars and nebulae.

youtube.title: Why is there MATTER at all?! 🤯🌌
